[[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, [[:en:Ōita District, Ōita| Ōita]] and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} *[https://oita-yusuhara.com Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Bungo Province]] [[Category:Ōita (city)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] }} [[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} [[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:柞原八幡宮]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]] [[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q195715}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q195715}}

From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web [[commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, [[:en:Ōita District, Ōita| Ōita]] and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. [[commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, [[:en:Ōita District, Ōita| Ōita]] and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} *[https://oita-yusuhara.com Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} *[https://oita-yusuhara.com Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, [[:en:Ōita District, Ōita| Ōita]] and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, [[:en:Ōita District, Ōita| Ōita]] and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] from the time they entered the province as ''[[:en:Shugo|Shugo]]'' during the Kamakura period. In the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], it was destroyed by [[:en:Ōtomo Sōrin|Ōtomo Sōrin]] after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Funai Domain|Funai Domain]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''[[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphora officinarum]]'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ===[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''[[:en:Halberd|Halberd]]''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} *[https://oita-yusuhara.com Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, {{ill|Ōita District, Ōita|en|Ōita District, Ōita|WD=Q8081521|lt= Ōita}} and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. The shrine was supported by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} from the time they entered the province as ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}}'' during the Kamakura period. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was destroyed by {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|en|Ōtomo Sōrin|WD=Q1185032|lt=Ōtomo Sōrin}} after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Funai Domain|en|Funai Domain|WD=Q3034441|lt=Funai Domain}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''{{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphora officinarum}}'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ==={{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''{{ill|Halberd|en|Halberd|WD=Q185623|lt=Halberd}}''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, {{ill|Ōita District, Ōita|en|Ōita District, Ōita|WD=Q8081521|lt= Ōita}} and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} from the time they entered the province as ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}}'' during the Kamakura period. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was destroyed by {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|en|Ōtomo Sōrin|WD=Q1185032|lt=Ōtomo Sōrin}} after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Funai Domain|en|Funai Domain|WD=Q3034441|lt=Funai Domain}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''{{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphora officinarum}}'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ==={{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''{{ill|Halberd|en|Halberd|WD=Q185623|lt=Halberd}}''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q195715]] Wikidata name: Yusuhara Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, {{ill|Ōita District, Ōita|en|Ōita District, Ōita|WD=Q8081521|lt= Ōita}} and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} from the time they entered the province as ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}}'' during the Kamakura period. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was destroyed by {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|en|Ōtomo Sōrin|WD=Q1185032|lt=Ōtomo Sōrin}} after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Funai Domain|en|Funai Domain|WD=Q3034441|lt=Funai Domain}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''{{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphora officinarum}}'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ==={{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''{{ill|Halberd|en|Halberd|WD=Q185623|lt=Halberd}}''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== 柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, {{ill|Ōita District, Ōita|en|Ōita District, Ōita|WD=Q8081521|lt= Ōita}} and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} from the time they entered the province as ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}}'' during the Kamakura period. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was destroyed by {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|en|Ōtomo Sōrin|WD=Q1185032|lt=Ōtomo Sōrin}} after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Funai Domain|en|Funai Domain|WD=Q3034441|lt=Funai Domain}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''{{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphora officinarum}}'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ==={{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yusuhara-hachimangu]][[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]][[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[ja:柞原八幡宮]][[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]][[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Ōita, Ōita, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yusuhara Hachiman-gū
柞原八幡宮 | image = YusuharaHachimangu Shaden.JPG | caption = ''Shaden'' of Yusuhara Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|14|18.2|N|131|33|3.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = c.830 | date_destroyed = | location = 987 Yawata, Ōita-shi, Ōita-ken | website = {{Official|1=https://oita-yusuhara.com}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = March 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gū'''|柞原八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita | Ōita]], {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, the other being the [[Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Jinja]], also in Ōita.[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually on March 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} It is also sometimes referred to as {{nihongo|'''Yusubara Hachiman-gū'''|由原八幡宮||}}. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''[[Hachiman]]'''|八幡||}}, here identified as the trinity of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend, which dates from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} states that in the year 827, a monk of [[Enryaku-ji]] named Konki worshipped at [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] for 1000 days and received an oracle which stated that the ''kami'' Hachiman would bless Bungo Province. A mysterious white banner flew over Kakugo, {{ill|Ōita District, Ōita|en|Ōita District, Ōita|WD=Q8081521|lt= Ōita}} and was presented to [[:en:Emperor Ninmyo|Emperor Ninmyo]], who ordered the governor of Bungo Province to construct this shrine. From the year 998 onwards, it became the practice to reconstruct the shrine buildings at 33 year intervals, the same as at Usa Hachiman-gū. Despite its dispute with Sasumuta Jinja, which as an equally ancient claim to the title, it has been referred to as the "Bungo ''ichinomiya'' since the 12th century, because of the shrine's proximity to the ''[[kokufu]]'' of Bungo Province. The shrine was supported by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} from the time they entered the province as ''{{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}}'' during the Kamakura period. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, it was destroyed by {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|en|Ōtomo Sōrin|WD=Q1185032|lt=Ōtomo Sōrin}} after his conversion to Christianity, but was rebuilt in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} and supported by the successive ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Funai Domain|en|Funai Domain|WD=Q3034441|lt=Funai Domain}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. In 1912, it was promoted to a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 3rd Rank|国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} .{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Yusuhara hachimangu Big gate.JPG|Torii Gate Higurashimon.JPG|Nandaimon Yusuhara hachimangu Nishimon.JPG|Nishimon Yusuhara hachimangu Roumon.JPG|'Moshidono, Heiden, Rōmon Inside Roumon.JPG|Inside the shrine buildings ==Cultural properties== ===Natural Monuments=== * ''{{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphora officinarum}}'', National Natural Monument.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2851|title=柞原八幡宮のクス|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} Claimed to be over 3000 years old. Mentioned by [[:en:Ino Tadataka|Ino Tadataka]] in his 1811 survey of Japan ==={{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}=== *'''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu''' (10 structures), Edo Period.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/00004531|title=柞原八幡宮|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ** Honden, 1850 ** Mōshidono, 1752 ** Haiden, 1759 ** Rōmon, 1760 ** Tōhōden, 1757 ** Saihōden, 1757 ** East Corridor, 1798 ** West Corridor, 1798 ** West Gate, late Edo Period ** South Great Gate, 1870, with storehouse including 4 documents: blueprints for Main Shrine and Rōmon, 2 maps of precincts) * '''[[Tachi]] sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Kunimune"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6405|title=太刀〈銘国宗／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Tachi sword''', Kamakura period, inscribed "Minamoto no Kuni?"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6404|title=太刀〈銘源国（以下一字竝年号不明）／〉〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''{{ill|Halberd|en|Halberd|WD=Q185623|lt=Halberd}}''', Nanboku-chō period, inscribed "Kunishige" and "Hachiman Daibodhisattva Tenma Daijizai Tenjin"{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6406|title=薙刀直シ刀〈銘国重八幡大菩薩天満大自在天神／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Bronze Buddha statue''', {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/4648 |title=銅造仏像（社伝阿弥陀如來立像）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} *'''Armor''', complete set, Muromachi period, {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/7221 |title=白檀塗浅葱糸威腹巻〈兜・大袖・小具足付／〉|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} * '''Yusuhara Hachiman-gu old documents''', Heian period, 17 scrolls with 216 documents. {{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10379 |title=柞原八幡宮文書（二百十六通）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} *[https://oita-yusuhara.com Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Yusuhara Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Yusuhara-hachimangu}} [[fr:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:柞原八幡宮]] [[simple:Yusuhara Hachiman-gū]] [[zh:柞原八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q195715}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bungo Province]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Ōita (city)]]
